name: TALKING HEAD
desc: Person speaking directly to camera. The most scalable and versatile format in paid social.

subs.name: Simple Testimonial
subs.quando: Social proof. Bottom funnel, retargeting audiences.
subs.estrutura: Intro → pain they had → how they found it → specific result → recommendation
subs.hook: "I tried everything for [result]. Nothing worked until I found this."
subs.dica: Phone camera, natural light, real environment. The rawer it looks, the better it converts.

subs.name: Car Testimonial
subs.quando: Top/mid funnel. The car creates maximum authenticity — feels like a real confession.
subs.estrutura: Phone on dashboard inside the car → talks about the product like calling a friend
subs.hook: "I'm sitting in my car because I had to tell you this right now…"
subs.dica: Don't edit the setting. Street noise, natural window light — it all adds authenticity.

subs.name: Hot Take / Controversy
subs.quando: Scroll interruption. Generates comments and organic reach.
subs.estrutura: Controversial claim → dramatic pause → context that justifies it → product as proof
subs.hook: "[Counterintuitive claim about your niche]. Yeah, I said it."
subs.dica: Polarizing is the strategy. People who disagree comment — and comments are distribution.

subs.name: Founder / Expert Cam
subs.quando: New products, high price points, brand authority.
subs.estrutura: "Hi, I'm [name], I started [X] because…" → market problem → different solution → invite
subs.hook: "There's one thing no one in my industry talks about openly. I'm going to tell you."
subs.dica: The founder doesn't need to look polished. Authenticity beats production every time.

subs.name: Objection Handler Q&A
subs.quando: Bottom funnel. For people who know but haven't bought yet.
subs.estrutura: Objection appears on screen → address it directly → flip the argument → CTA
subs.hook: "You're probably thinking: [real objection]. Let me explain."
subs.dica: Use real objections from comments and DMs — the more specific, the more powerful.

subs.name: Testimonial Mashup
subs.quando: Social proof at volume. Overcomes objections at scale.
subs.estrutura: Fast cuts of 4–8 people with different results → unified CTA
subs.hook: No spoken hook. The first cut with a real result IS the hook.
subs.dica: Each person: 3–6 seconds. Varied results create credibility through volume.

name: FAKE PODCAST
desc: Simulates a viral podcast clip. Maximum credibility — the brain processes it as interesting content, not advertising.

subs.name: Two Hosts Discussing the Product
subs.quando: Products that need context. Any niche.
subs.estrutura: Two presenters 'conversing' → one doesn't know the product → the other reveals it → surprise reaction
subs.hook: "— Dude, do you actually use this every day? — Every single day. Can't live without it."
subs.dica: Table, microphones (even fake ones), neutral background. The podcast aesthetic alone sells credibility.

subs.name: Expert Solo Clip (Podcast Cut)
subs.quando: Authority + education. Works in business, health, finance niches.
subs.estrutura: Clip 'extracted from a podcast' → expert explains something important → product is the logical conclusion
subs.hook: "This is what I'd tell anyone who wants [result]."
subs.dica: Add a podcast progress bar on screen. That detail alone dramatically increases perceived authenticity.

subs.name: Podcast + Proof of Results
subs.quando: Conversion. Combines authority with social proof.
subs.estrutura: Podcast format → interviewer asks 'how did you get there?' → guest tells story → result
subs.hook: "— So when did you realize you'd found something different? — When I saw this number."
subs.dica: The number or result appears as an overlay on screen while the guest speaks.

name: NEWS FORMAT
desc: Journalistic aesthetic. Instant authority through format alone. The brain processes it as information, not an ad.

subs.name: Breaking News Style
subs.quando: Top of funnel. Launches, new discoveries, niche updates.
subs.estrutura: News ticker on screen + serious anchor → 'new discovery in [niche]' → product is the revelation
subs.hook: "BREAKING: New study changes everything we knew about [niche topic]."
subs.dica: News-style caption font, studio background (or green screen). No product logo at the start.

subs.name: Real News Commentary
subs.quando: Third-party authority. Real news validates the product indirectly.
subs.estrutura: Real headline/news appears on screen → live commentary → connects to product as the solution
subs.hook: "I saw this headline today and had to comment immediately."
subs.dica: Green screen with the headline as background. Person in front, pointing to the data.

subs.name: Fact / Fun Fact Style
subs.quando: Education + curiosity. Works for supplements, apps, services with a strong stat.
subs.estrutura: 'Did you know [surprising niche fact]?' → visual data → implication → product as the answer
subs.hook: "Did you know [specific, shocking stat]? Let me explain why that matters."
subs.dica: The stat must be real and verifiable. A false data point destroys the brand if discovered.

name: INTERVIEW / STREET
desc: Journalistic format with real people on camera. Native feel, organic, generates immediate curiosity.

subs.name: Expert Interview
subs.quando: Authority + depth. Works in health, finance, marketing niches.
subs.estrutura: Interviewer asks question → expert answers with depth → product emerges naturally
subs.hook: "I brought [expert] here to explain something I've always wanted to understand about [topic]."
subs.dica: Two microphones, fixed camera, interview framing. No expensive studio needed.

subs.name: Street Interview
subs.quando: Native social proof. Looks like organic content — high scroll-stop rate.
subs.estrutura: Question for people on the street → varied answers → transition to the product
subs.hook: "I stopped 10 random people and asked them [provocative niche question]."
subs.dica: Handheld camera, real environment. Each answer: 3–5 seconds. Dynamism is what retains.

subs.name: Self-Interview / Comment Q&A
subs.quando: Engagement + conversion. Answering real questions builds trust.
subs.estrutura: Real comment question appears on screen → direct answer → CTA
subs.hook: "Someone asked in the comments: [real question]. Let me answer that right now."
subs.dica: Use the question as a screen overlay. Give a direct answer, zero filler.

name: SKIT
desc: Short dramatized scene. One or multiple characters. High watch time, organic feel, works with comedy.

subs.name: Solo Skit (One Actor, Two Characters)
subs.quando: Fashion, beauty, lifestyle, education. High watch time and conversion.
subs.estrutura: One person plays two characters — 'without the product' vs 'with it' — side by side or in cuts
subs.hook: The scene IS the hook. No spoken opener — immediate action.
subs.dica: Fast cut between the two characters. Outfit or position change is enough to differentiate. Intentional humor.

subs.name: Problem–Solution Dramatized
subs.quando: Any niche. Exaggerated real problem → product solves it.
subs.estrutura: Scene of real frustration (exaggerated) → turning point → product as solution → relief reaction
subs.hook: The frustration scene IS the hook — say nothing in the first 2 seconds.
subs.dica: Controlled exaggeration is the key. Too much becomes comedy; too little doesn't retain.

subs.name: The Skeptic Converted
subs.quando: Overcome disbelief. Very effective for products with strong objections.
subs.estrutura: Skeptical character ridicules the product → uses it → gets surprised → recommends it to a friend
subs.hook: "I said I would never use this. Look at me now."
subs.dica: The transformation must be visible and fast. Don't drag the skepticism past the halfway mark.

name: POV
desc: Point of View. Places the viewer inside the situation. Instant immersion and identification.

subs.name: POV: Relatable Situation
subs.quando: Identification with everyday pain or desire.
subs.estrutura: 'POV: [frustrating everyday situation]' → lives through it → product solves it
subs.hook: "POV: you're staring at the ads manager watching money burn."
subs.dica: The more specific the situation, the more powerful. Avoid generic, vague POVs.

subs.name: POV: Already Got the Result
subs.quando: Activates imagination of desired state. Mid/bottom funnel.
subs.estrutura: 'POV: you already [result the avatar wants]' → shows that possible world → CTA
subs.hook: "POV: you woke up and your account had sales notifications from while you slept."
subs.dica: Show sales apps, notifications, the 'after' lifestyle. Make it sensory.

subs.name: POV: Before vs. After
subs.quando: Transformation contrast. First-person narrative.
subs.estrutura: 'POV: me [X time] ago' → bad situation → 'POV: me today' → visual transformation
subs.hook: "POV: me 6 months ago, with no idea where to start."
subs.dica: Cut midway between the two POVs. The visual change must be immediately clear.

name: QUESTION BOX
desc: Simulates native Stories/TikTok Q&A format. Organic feel — looks like content, not an ad.

subs.name: Real Follower Question
subs.quando: Looks like organic content. High retention.
subs.estrutura: Question box sticker with real question → answers on camera → CTA at the end
subs.hook: The question on screen IS the hook. It must name a real, specific pain point.
subs.dica: Visual identical to the native Instagram sticker. Don't use generic, made-up questions.

subs.name: Rapid-Fire Question Series
subs.quando: Cumulative identification. Each question is an avatar pain point.
subs.estrutura: 3–5 questions in sequence → each one = a pain → single CTA at the end
subs.hook: "Do you [pain 1]? Do you [pain 2]? Do you [pain 3]? Then this video is for you."
subs.dica: Build intensity with each question — the last one should be the most specific.

subs.name: Question + Surprise Answer
subs.quando: Cognitive dissonance. Answer goes against common sense.
subs.estrutura: Innocent question → unexpected answer → explains the paradox → product
subs.hook: "What's the #1 mistake people make trying to lose weight?" → "Exercise." (explained after)
subs.dica: The paradox alone holds the viewer even if they want to leave.

name: REACTION
desc: Live reaction to something. Authentic emotion + proof = conversion.

subs.name: Reacting to Own Results
subs.quando: Authentic social proof. Hard to fake or look forced.
subs.estrutura: 'I'm going to check my results live' → genuine reaction → context → CTA
subs.hook: "Let me open this right now…" [pause] "…wow."
subs.dica: Don't edit the reaction. The shock IS the creative asset.

subs.name: Duet / Stitch Style
subs.quando: Native to TikTok. Generates curiosity about the original clip.
subs.estrutura: Split screen: third-party clip + your reaction next to it → commentary leads to product
subs.hook: The original clip is already the visual hook. Your reaction creates the tension.
subs.dica: Use a clip from someone the avatar already knows. Familiarity amplifies retention.

subs.name: Reacting to a Hater's Comment
subs.quando: Controlled controversy. High retention through indignation.
subs.estrutura: Negative comment on screen → responds with data and calm → product proves the point
subs.hook: "This comment someone sent me. I'm going to respond right here, right now."
subs.dica: Respond with confidence, never aggression. The product wins the argument.

name: SPLIT SCREEN
desc: Side-by-side comparison. The most universal contrast format — works across every niche and funnel stage.

subs.name: Before / After
subs.quando: The most universal conversion format in the history of advertising.
subs.estrutura: Side A = before (visual pain) | Side B = after (result) → CTA
subs.hook: "[N] days difference. Look."
subs.dica: The more visually striking the difference, the higher the impact. Don't leave it subtle.

subs.name: Right vs. Wrong
subs.quando: Educates and positions simultaneously.
subs.estrutura: Side A = wrong way (what most do) | Side B = right way (your method)
subs.hook: "90% do it this way ❌. The 10% who scale do it like this ✅."
subs.dica: Bold ❌ and ✅ emojis to guide fast reading.

subs.name: What I Ordered vs. What I Got
subs.quando: E-commerce and physical products. Overcomes online buying skepticism.
subs.estrutura: 'Expected [expectation]' vs 'Got [better reality]' → positive surprise
subs.hook: "I was nervous to order because…" → opens product → positive surprise
subs.dica: Set expectations low early so the positive reality hits harder.

name: NATIVE UGC
desc: Looks like an organic post, not an ad. Maximum retention — the viewer doesn't feel sold to.

subs.name: Honest Review
subs.quando: Builds trust by not looking forced.
subs.estrutura: Expectation → real usage → positives → 1 honest negative → favorable conclusion
subs.hook: "I'm going to be really honest about [product]. There are good things and one thing I didn't like."
subs.dica: 1–2 minor negatives dramatically increase the credibility of the positives.

subs.name: "Saw It on TikTok" / FOMO Format
subs.quando: FOMO-driven products. Ideal for trends and launches.
subs.estrutura: 'I've seen this product everywhere on TikTok' → skeptical → tested → surprised → recommends
subs.hook: "OK, I finally bought the thing everyone keeps talking about. I'll be honest."
subs.dica: Authentic initial skepticism. The turn is more powerful when the skepticism was genuine.

subs.name: GRWM / Day in My Life
subs.quando: Beauty, fashion, lifestyle, health. Native product integration.
subs.estrutura: Prep routine → product appears naturally in use → result → soft CTA
subs.hook: "Getting ready for [event] with you. There's a new product I'm kind of obsessed with."
subs.dica: Intimate conversation. Product isn't the protagonist at the start — it appears as a natural detail.

subs.name: Unboxing / Reveal
subs.quando: Generates anticipation and desire for the product.
subs.estrutura: Package arrives → opens live → reveals in parts → reaction → initial use
subs.hook: "Just arrived. Opening it right now, live."
subs.dica: Progressive pace. Don't reveal everything at once — extend the anticipation deliberately.

name: GREEN SCREEN
desc: Person in front of an image or video background. Visual context without needing to be physically there.

subs.name: Green Screen with Results Screenshot
subs.quando: Proof + authority. Result is visible while you're explaining it.
subs.estrutura: Screenshot as background → person in front → points and comments → CTA
subs.hook: "This result came in on Monday morning." [background = screenshot]
subs.dica: Point to specific elements in the screenshot while speaking.

subs.name: Green Screen with News / Study
subs.quando: Third-party authority. 'It's not me saying it, it's the science/media.'
subs.estrutura: Headline or study as background → live commentary → connects to product
subs.hook: "I saw this news today and had to comment immediately." [background = headline]
subs.dica: Background legible but slightly blurred. Person in foreground well-lit.

subs.name: Green Screen with Comment
subs.quando: Controlled controversy. Response that flips the argument.
subs.estrutura: Comment (good or bad) as background → responds → product is the proof
subs.hook: "This comment right here deserves a real response."
subs.dica: Both hater and fan comments work. Hater comments = more retentive.

name: NARRATION / VOICEOVER
desc: Narration audio over images or text. Scale without needing to be on camera.

subs.name: Narration over B-Roll
subs.quando: High perceived production value, low actual production cost.
subs.estrutura: Context images → narrator's voice telling story/problem/solution over them
subs.hook: The first 2s of image + start of narration = dual simultaneous hook.
subs.dica: B-roll synced with what's being said. Cut every 2–3 seconds.

subs.name: Kinetic Text (Animated Text)
subs.quando: High visual impact. Dramatic sounds. Works for feed with sound off.
subs.estrutura: Text animates in synced with narration → dark background
subs.hook: The first word on screen IS the visual hook. Make it high-impact.
subs.dica: One word at a time or short phrases. Speed synced with audio.

subs.name: VSL (Video Sales Letter)
subs.quando: Bottom funnel. Long-form copy presented as video. High conversion for mid/high ticket.
subs.estrutura: Strong hook → problem → agitation → revelation → solution → proof → offer → CTA
subs.hook: "If you [specific situation], then this video was made for you."
subs.dica: Calm voice, simple slides, no camera needed. 3–15 minutes for cold traffic.

name: STORYTELLING
desc: Transformation narrative. Emotional hook that holds attention from the first second to the CTA.

subs.name: The Hero's Journey
subs.quando: Deep emotional connection. Works long and short form.
subs.estrutura: Life before (pain) → turning point → journey → life now → invitation
subs.hook: "[X time] ago I was [bad situation]. Today my life is completely different."
subs.dica: The opening pain must be specific and visual. The avatar has to see themselves in it.

subs.name: Client Success Story
subs.quando: System proof. Builds trust through third-party narrative.
subs.estrutura: Who they were → what they tried before → how they found it → specific result
subs.hook: "[Name] came to me [situation]. In [X] days, everything changed."
subs.dica: Result with a specific number. Not 'improved' but 'grew 340% in 30 days'.

subs.name: The Revealing Failure
subs.quando: Intentional vulnerability. Trust built through radical honesty.
subs.estrutura: Admits real mistake → lesson learned → how they changed → product born from it
subs.hook: "I'm going to tell you my biggest mistake that cost me $X — because it might save you."
subs.dica: The failure must be real. A fabricated failure feels manipulative and people can tell.

name: LISTICLE / TOP N
desc: Numbered list of tips, mistakes, or secrets. Generates expectation and retention through completeness.

subs.name: "X Mistakes You're Making"
subs.quando: Top of funnel. Pain of incompetence → product corrects it.
subs.estrutura: Announces N mistakes → fast list (each mistake = identification) → product solves all of them
subs.hook: "5 mistakes keeping you from [result] — #3 is the most common."
subs.dica: The most relatable mistake should be #2 or #3 — not the last. Keeps attention longer.

subs.name: "X Reasons To"
subs.quando: Rational justification for buying. Works as a pre-sell.
subs.estrutura: '3 reasons why [product/method] is different' → reason by reason → CTA
subs.hook: "I'll give you 3 reasons why you're going to stop wasting time on [alternative]."
subs.dica: Short and direct. Each reason: max 5 seconds of explanation.

subs.name: "X Secrets Nobody Tells You"
subs.quando: Curiosity + exclusivity. Triggers sense of privileged access.
subs.estrutura: 'X secrets that [reference group] uses but doesn't share' → reveals → product
subs.hook: "The top [niche] players use these 3 secrets. Here's what they are."
subs.dica: Secrets must be real and useful. No empty clickbait.

name: DEMO
desc: Shows the product working live. 'Seeing is believing' is the most powerful trigger in any format.

subs.name: Live Demo (Screen Recording)
subs.quando: Digital products, apps, tools. Concrete proof of functionality.
subs.estrutura: 'I'm going to show you live' → real screen recording → visible result → CTA
subs.hook: "Opening my screen right now. Watch me [result] in [time]."
subs.dica: Don't over-edit the demo. Minor mistakes humanize it and increase credibility.

subs.name: Full Tutorial (Value + Product)
subs.quando: Reciprocity. Genuinely teaches something → product is the upgrade.
subs.estrutura: 'In [N] steps I'll show you how to [result]' → actually teaches → product accelerates
subs.hook: "In 4 minutes I'll show you how to [result] from scratch."
subs.dica: Deliver real value. The product is the upgrade, not the gatekeeper of knowledge.

subs.name: ASMR / Sensory Demo
subs.quando: Physical products. High engagement through curiosity and sensory appeal.
subs.estrutura: Product sounds in use → visual close-up → text on screen explains → CTA
subs.hook: Close-up image + unexpected product sound = scroll stop.
subs.dica: No narration. The product speaks for itself. Minimal text on screen.

name: STATIC / IMAGE
desc: Static image or with light animation. Fast to produce, testable at volume — massively underrated.

subs.name: Product + Strong Headline
subs.quando: Brand recognition and e-commerce. Bottom of funnel.
subs.estrutura: Product highlighted + specific headline + clear visual CTA
subs.hook: The headline in the image IS the hook. Unique, specific, irresistible.
subs.dica: Solid or textured background. Product as the hero. No visual clutter.

subs.name: Grid / Collage of Results
subs.quando: Social proof volume in a single image.
subs.estrutura: 2x2 or 3x3 with screenshots of results from different customers
subs.hook: '[N] real results. [Month/Year].' — the caption does the heavy lifting.
subs.dica: Uniform color palette. Organized screenshots create a sense of proven system.

subs.name: Adapted Meme
subs.quando: Organic reach. Breaks the visual pattern of standard ads.
subs.estrutura: Recognizable meme template + niche situation → product as the punchline
subs.hook: The meme IS the hook. The niche recognizes the template and stops scrolling.
subs.dica: Only memes the avatar saw in the last 7 days. A stale meme doesn't stop the scroll.

subs.name: Comparison Table
subs.quando: Logic and data. Destroys objections visually.
subs.estrutura: Column 'the market' vs column 'your product' → ❌ and ✅
subs.hook: "Why [N] people are switching to [product]."
subs.dica: Table items must be real. Never invent false advantages.

name: TREND HIJACK
desc: Uses viral audio, format, or meme of the moment. The algorithm already distributes it — you just fit your message in.

subs.name: Trending Audio + Product
subs.quando: Maximum organic reach. Algorithm favors trending audio.
subs.estrutura: Trending audio → product images/scenes synced to it → text reinforces message
subs.hook: The trendy audio itself is the hook. Familiarity stops the scroll.
subs.dica: TikTok Creative Center for trending audio. Sync audio peaks with visual peaks.

subs.name: "People Always Ask Me" Format
subs.quando: Specific TikTok trend format. Naturally viral structure.
subs.estrutura: 'People always ask me, [name], how do you [result]? I don't know about X but I know [product]'
subs.hook: "People always ask me how I get [desired result]."
subs.dica: Follow the exact meme structure. Subvert expectations at the end with the product.

subs.name: Native Feed Content (No Ad Feel)
subs.quando: Maximum retention. No logo, no obvious CTA at the start.
subs.estrutura: Opens like a normal post → delivers real value → soft CTA at the end
subs.hook: "3 things I learned [result] — no exaggeration."
subs.dica: In the first 5 seconds: zero branding, zero logo, zero CTA. Must look organic.

name: URGENCY / DIRECT OFFER
desc: Bottom-funnel formats. Scarcity, price, bonuses. For warm audiences who already know you.

subs.name: Real Scarcity
subs.quando: Purchase decision trigger. Retargeting campaigns.
subs.estrutura: Exact number of spots/time remaining → what changes after → urgent CTA
subs.hook: "Cart closes in 6 hours. 47 spots total and 31 are already gone."
subs.dica: Only use real scarcity. Fake scarcity permanently kills trust in the brand.

subs.name: Bonus Stack
subs.quando: Increases perceived value before checkout.
subs.estrutura: 'Besides the product you also get…' → bonus list with values → total price vs offer price
subs.hook: "Besides [main product], you get [bonus 1], [bonus 2], [bonus 3]. All for [price]."
subs.dica: Bonus values must be real and credible. Don't inflate them artificially.

subs.name: Price Anchoring
subs.quando: Product perceived as expensive. Price objection retargeting.
subs.estrutura: 'This costs less than [daily reference]' → compares real value vs price → CTA
subs.hook: "$1 a day. Less than a coffee. And it could change your career."
subs.dica: The comparison reference should be something the avatar buys without thinking.

subs.name: Guarantee as Hook
subs.quando: Eliminate perceived risk. Works best as the first argument, not a footnote.
subs.estrutura: 'If it doesn't work, I'll refund 100%' → context → benefits → CTA
subs.hook: "Buy it, use it for 7 days. If you don't love it, I'll refund every dollar. No questions asked."
subs.dica: Guarantee at the start, not the end. Inverts the logic of perceived risk.
